original: You dodge out of its path.
You taste iron, and you’re knocked to a place where it seems as if the battle is coming from a long way away...
You manage to sidestep the dart.
The dart breaks apart on your shield.
The dart pricks into you, releasing its lusty, woozy payload.
You manage to wrench yourself out of his grip.
You can’t stop yourself being pulled down in turn! The remaining zil cheer and close in, scenting victory.
You duck out of the way.
It splatters across you, and you groan as the scent and flavor of it works itself into your head...

From left to right is a cornucopia of frenzied insectile lust, and it’s difficult not to be overwhelmed by it. It seems to have only been opened in front of you for a few mere moments though before a wave of freshly relieved wasp fighters are back on their feet and charging back into the fray, triumphantly resuming that deafening hum-cry of theirs.

Every member that can raise a blade, bow or blunt instrument in anger, male and female alike is here. The flames of their torches glimmer off their black armor, beetle-like oval shields and the sticky war paint they have smeared across their wild, tar-eyed faces. You’ve backed yourself against the wall of a wax building so you don’t have to fight literally every single one of them at once, but the lithe bee people are a thicket of yellow, tar and flint all around you, closing off any notion of escape. A buzzing ululation is on all of their lips, a rising and falling war cry that seems to inspire them and drive them to frenzy, fearlessly flying at you regardless of where you’ve got your weapon aimed.\n\nThe ones that can’t reach you stamp their boots in the sand or fly above the foray, droning encouragement to those that can, eagerly awaiting their own opportunity to hurl themselves at the land-stealer champion.
